    3. Python 中的卡尔曼滤波与贝叶斯滤波 05-多元高斯分布.ipynb

Table of Contents  目录

Multivariate Gaussians  多元高斯分布¶

Modeling Uncertainty in Multiple Dimensions
多维度下的不确定性建模

In [1]:  在[1]中：
%matplotlib inline
In [2]:  在[2]中：
#format the book
import book_format
book_format.set_style()
Out[2]:  Out[2]：

Introduction  引言¶

The techniques in the last chapter are very powerful, but they only work with one variable or dimension. They provide no way to represent multidimensional data, such as the position and velocity of a dog in a field. Position and velocity are related to each other, and as we learned in the g-h chapter we should never throw away information. In this chapter we learn how to describe this relationship probabilistically. Through this key insight we will achieve markedly better filter performance.
上一章中介绍的各种技术虽然非常有效，但它们只能处理单个变量或维度的数据。这些技术无法用来表示多维数据，比如一只狗在田野中的位置和速度。位置和速度之间存在着相互关联的关系。正如我们在 g-h 章节中学到的，我们绝不能丢弃任何有用的信息。在这一章中，我们将学习如何用概率论的方式来描述这种相互关系。通过这一重要思路，我们将能够显著提升过滤器的性能。

Multivariate Normal Distributions
多元正态分布¶

We've been using Gaussians for a scalar random variable, expressed as N(μ,σ2)N(μ,σ2). A more formal term for this is univariate normal, where univariate means 'one variable'. The probability distribution of the Gaussian is known as the univariate normal distribution.
对于标量随机变量，我们一直使用高斯分布来描述，其数学表达式为 N(μ,σ2)N(μ,σ2) 。更正式的称谓是“单变量正态分布”，其中“单变量”指的是只有一个变量。高斯分布所对应的概率分布，就被称为单变量正态分布。

What might a multivariate normal distribution be? Multivariate means multiple variables. Our goal is to be able to represent a normal distribution with multiple dimensions. I don't necessarily mean spatial dimensions - if we track the position, velocity, and acceleration of an aircraft in (x, y, z) that gives us a nine dimensional problem. Consider a two dimensional case. It might be the x and y coordinates of a robot, it might be the position and velocity of a dog on the x-axis, or milk production and feed rate at a dairy. It doesn't really matter. We can see that for NN dimensions, we need NN means, which we will arrange in a column matrix (vector) like so:
什么是多元正态分布呢？所谓“多元”，指的是多个变量。我们的目标是能够用多维的方式来表示正态分布。这里所说的“维度”并不一定指空间维度——比如，如果我们记录一架飞机在(x, y, z)坐标系中的位置、速度和加速度，那就构成了一个九维的模型。以二维情况为例：可以是机器人的 x 和 y 坐标，也可以是狗在 x 轴上的位置和速度，又或者是奶牛场的牛奶产量和饲料消耗量。其实具体是什么变量并不重要。我们可以看出，对于 n 维的情况，我们需要 n 个均值，这些均值可以被排列成一个列矩阵或向量，如下所示：

μ=⎡⎣⎢⎢⎢⎢μ1μ2⋮μn⎤⎦⎥⎥⎥⎥μ=[μ1μ2⋮μn]

Let's say we believe that x=2x=2 and y=17y=17. We would have
假设我们相信 x=2x=2 和 y=17y=17 的说法。那么我们就会……

μ=[217]μ=[217]

The next step is representing our variances. At first blush we might think we would also need N variances for N dimensions. We might want to say the variance for x is 10 and the variance for y is 4, like so.
下一步是表示各个变量的方差。乍看之下，我们可能会认为，对于 N 个维度来说，需要 N 个方差值。比如，我们可以说 x 的方差是 10，y 的方差是 4。

σ2=[104]σ2=[104]

This is incomplete because it does not consider the more general case. In the Gaussians chapter we computed the variance in the heights of students. That is a measure of how the heights vary relative to each other. If all students are the same height, then the variance is 0, and if their heights are wildly different, then the variance will be large.
这个分析并不完整，因为它没有考虑到更一般的情况。在高斯分布的相关内容中，我们计算了学生身高的方差。方差实际上反映了学生们身高之间的差异程度。如果所有学生的身高都相同，那么方差为 0；而如果学生们的身高差异很大，那么方差就会很大。

There is also a relationship between height and weight. In general, a taller person weighs more than a shorter person. Height and weight are correlated. We want a way to express not only what we think the variance is in the height and the weight, but also the degree to which they are correlated. In other words, we want to know how weight varies compared to the heights. We call that the covariance.
身高和体重之间也存在一定的关系。一般来说，身高较高的人，体重也相对较重。身高和体重是相互关联的。我们希望找到一种方式来描述身高和体重的差异程度，以及它们之间的关联强度。换句话说，我们想知道体重是如何随着身高的变化而变化的。我们把这种关系称为协方差。

Before we can understand multivariate normal distributions we need to understand the mathematics behind correlations and covariances.
在理解多元正态分布之前，我们有必要先掌握与相关系数和协方差相关的数学知识。

Correlation and Covariance
相关性与协方差¶

Covariance describes how much two variables vary together. Covariance is short for correlated variances. In other words, variance is a measure for how a population vary amongst themselves, and covariance is a measure for how much two variables change in relation to each other. For example, as height increases weight also generally increases. These variables are correlated. They are positively correlated because as one variable gets larger so does the other. As the outdoor temperature decreases home heating bills increase. These are inversely correlated or negatively correlated because as one variable gets larger the other variable lowers. The price of tea and the number of tail wags my dog makes have no relation to each other, and we say they are uncorrelated or independent- each can change independent of the other.
协方差用来描述两个变量相互变化的程度。所谓协方差，其实就是“相关方差”的简称。换句话说，方差衡量的是整个数据群体内部的变动情况，而协方差则衡量的是两个变量之间相互变化的程度。例如，身高增加时，体重通常也会增加。这两个变量是相关的，且属于正相关关系——因为其中一个变量增大时，另一个变量也会随之增大。再比如，室外温度下降时，家庭取暖费用会上升。这两个变量则是负相关关系——因为其中一个变量增大时，另一个变量则减小。至于茶的价格和我的狗摇尾巴的次数，它们之间没有任何关联，我们可以说它们是不相关的或独立的——每个变量的变化都不受另一个变量的影响。

Correlation allows prediction. If you are significantly taller than me I can predict that you also weigh more than me. As winter comes I predict that I will be spending more to heat my house. If my dog wags his tail more I don't conclude that tea prices will be changing.
相关性有助于进行预测。如果你比我高很多，那么我可以推测你的体重也比我重。随着冬天的到来，我预计自己需要花费更多钱来供暖。不过，如果我的狗摇尾巴的频率增加了，我并不会因此就认为茶叶的价格会发生变化。

For example, here is a plot of height and weight of students on the school's track team. If a student is 68 inches tall I can predict they weigh roughly 160 pounds. Since the correlation is not perfect neither is my prediction.
例如，下图显示了该校田径队中各学生的身高和体重情况。如果某学生的身高为 68 英寸，那么可以推测他的体重约为 160 磅。不过，由于两者之间的关联并非完全精确，因此我的预测也未必完全准确。

In [3]:  在[3]中：
from kf_book.gaussian_internal import plot_correlated_data

height = [60, 62, 63, 65, 65.1, 68, 69, 70, 72, 74]
weight = [95, 120, 127, 119, 151, 143, 173, 171, 180, 210]
plot_correlated_data(height, weight, 'Height (in)', 'Weight (lbs)', False)

In this book we only consider linear correlation. We assume that the relationship between variables is linear. That is, a straight line is a good fit for the data. I've fit a straight line through the data in the above chart. The concept of nonlinear correlation exists, but we will not be using it.
在这本书中，我们只考虑线性相关性。我们假设各变量之间的关系是线性的。也就是说，用直线来描述这些数据是最合适的。我在上图中用直线来拟合了这些数据。虽然也存在非线性相关性的情况，但我们在本书中不会涉及这一概念。

The equation for the covariance between XX and YY is
XX 与 YY 之间的协方差公式为

COV(X,Y)=σxy=E[(X−μx)(Y−μy)]COV(X,Y)=σxy=E[(X−μx)(Y−μy)]

Where E[X]E[X] is the expected value of X, defined as
其中， E[X]E[X] 表示 X 的期望值，其定义如下：

E[X]={∑ni=1pixi∫∞−∞f(x)xdxdiscretecontinuousE[X]={∑i=1npixidiscrete∫−∞∞f(x)xdxcontinuous

We assume each data point is equally likely, so the probability of each is 1N1N, giving
我们假设每个数据点出现的可能性都是相等的，因此每个数据点被选中的概率都是 1N1N 。

E[X]=1N∑i=1nxiE[X]=1N∑i=1nxi

for the discrete case we will be considering.
对于我们即将考虑的离散情况而言。

Compare the covariance equation to the equation for the variance. As you can see they are very similar:
将协方差公式与方差公式进行比较。可以看出，两者非常相似：

VAR(X)=σ2xCOV(X,Y)=σxy=E[(X−μ)2]=E[(X−μx)(Y−μy)]VAR(X)=σx2=E[(X−μ)2]COV(X,Y)=σxy=E[(X−μx)(Y−μy)]

In particular, if you compute COV(X,X)COV(X,X) you get the equation for VAR(X)VAR(X), which supports my statement that the variance computes how a random variable varies amongst itself.
具体来说，如果你计算 COV(X,X)COV(X,X) ，就能得到 VAR(X)VAR(X) 的公式。这一结果进一步印证了我的观点：方差实际上反映了随机变量自身的变动情况。

We use a covariance matrix to denote covariances of a multivariate normal distribution, and it looks like this:
我们用协方差矩阵来表示多元正态分布中的各变量之间的协方差关系。其形式如下：

Σ=⎡⎣⎢⎢⎢⎢⎢σ21σ21⋮σn1σ12σ22⋮σn2⋯⋯⋱⋯σ1nσ2n⋮σ2n⎤⎦⎥⎥⎥⎥⎥Σ=[σ12σ12⋯σ1nσ21σ22⋯σ2n⋮⋮⋱⋮σn1σn2⋯σn2]

The diagonal contains the variance for each variable, and the off-diagonal elements contain the covariance between the ithith and jthjth variables. So σ23σ32 is the variance of the third variable, and σ13σ13 is the covariance between the first and third variables.
对角线上的数值表示各个变量的方差，而对角线以外的数值则表示第 ithith 个变量与第 jthjth 个变量之间的协方差。因此， σ23σ32 表示第三个变量的方差，而 σ13σ13 则表示第一个变量与第三个变量之间的协方差。

A covariance of 0 indicates no correlation. If the variance for xx is 10, the variance for yy is 4, and there is no linear correlation between xx and yy, then we would write
协方差为 0 表示两者之间没有相关性。如果 xx 的方差为 10， yy 的方差为 4，且 xx 与 yy 之间不存在线性相关性，那么我们可以这样表示：

Σ=[10004]Σ=[10004]

If there was a small amount of positive correlation between xx and yy we might have
如果 xx 和 yy 之间存在轻微的正相关关系的话，那或许……

Σ=[101.21.24]Σ=[101.21.24]

where 1.2 is the covariance between xx and yy. I say the correlation is "small" because the covariance of 1.2 is small relative to the variances of 10.
其中，1.2 表示 xx 与 yy 之间的协方差。我认为这种相关性“较弱”，因为 1.2 这个数值相对于 10 来说相当小。

If there was a large amount of negative correlation between between xx and yy we might have
如果 xx 与 yy 之间存在较大的负相关关系，那么可能会出现以下情况：

Σ=[10−9.7−9.74]Σ=[10−9.7−9.74]

The covariance matrix is symmetric. After all, the covariance between xx and yy is always equal to the covariance between yy and xx. That is, σxy=σyxσxy=σyx for any xx and yy.
协方差矩阵是对称的。毕竟， xx 与 yy 之间的协方差始终等于 yy 与 xx 之间的协方差。也就是说，对于任意的 xx 和 yy ，都有 σxy=σyxσxy=σyx 成立。

I fear I might be losing you, so let's work an example. In the Gaussians chapter we had a class of students with heights H=[1.8, 2.0, 1.7, 1.9, 1.6] meters. We computed:
恐怕我可能会失去你，所以让我们通过一个例子来说明吧。在高斯分布的例子中，我们有一组学生的身高分别为 H=[1.8, 2.0, 1.7, 1.9, 1.6]米。我们进行了如下计算：

VAR(H)=E[(H−μH)2]=1N∑i=1n(Hi−μH)2=15[(1.8−1.8)2+(2−1.8)2+(1.7−1.8)2+(1.9−1.8)2+(1.6−1.8)2]=0.02VAR(H)=E[(H−μH)2]=1N∑i=1n(Hi−μH)2=15[(1.8−1.8)2+(2−1.8)2+(1.7−1.8)2+(1.9−1.8)2+(1.6−1.8)2]=0.02

Easy, right? If we weigh the students we might find their weights to be W = [70.1, 91.2, 59.5, 93.2, 53.5]. Can we use the covariance equation to create the covariance matrix? Sure. It will look like:
很简单，对吧？如果我们称量这些学生的体重，可能会得到如下数值：W = [70.1, 91.2, 59.5, 93.2, 53.5]。那么，我们能否利用协方差公式来计算出协方差矩阵呢？当然可以。计算出来的协方差矩阵如下：

Σ=[σ2HσW,HσH,Wσ2W]Σ=[σH2σH,WσW,HσW2]

We just computed the variance of the height, and it will go in the upper left hand corner of the matrix. The lower right corner contains the variance in weights. Using the same equation we get:
我们刚刚计算出了身高的方差，该数值会出现在矩阵的左上角。右下角则包含体重的方差。利用同样的公式，我们可以得到：

μWσ2W=15(70.1+91.2+59.5+93.2+53.5)=73.5=15[(70.1−73.5)2+(91.2−73.5)2+(59.5−73.5)2+(93.2−73.5)2+(53.5−73.5)2]=261.8μW=15(70.1+91.2+59.5+93.2+53.5)=73.5σW2=15[(70.1−73.5)2+(91.2−73.5)2+(59.5−73.5)2+(93.2−73.5)2+(53.5−73.5)2]=261.8

Now the covariances. Using the formula above, we compute:
现在来计算协方差。利用上述公式，我们可以得到：

σH,W=E[(H−μH)(W−μW)]=1N∑i=1n(Hi−μH)(Wi−μW)=15[(1.8−1.8)(70.1−73.5)+(2−1.8)(91.2−73.5)+(1.7−1.8)(59.5−73.5)+(1.9−1.8)(93.2−73.5)+(1.6−1.8)(53.5−73.5)]=2.18σH,W=E[(H−μH)(W−μW)]=1N∑i=1n(Hi−μH)(Wi−μW)=15[(1.8−1.8)(70.1−73.5)+(2−1.8)(91.2−73.5)+(1.7−1.8)(59.5−73.5)+(1.9−1.8)(93.2−73.5)+(1.6−1.8)(53.5−73.5)]=2.18

That was tedious, but easy enough. We will never do that again because, of course, NumPy will compute it for you.
那虽然很繁琐，但其实也挺简单的。我们再也不会这么做啦，因为 NumPy 当然会帮我们完成这项计算工作。

In [4]:  在[4]中：
import numpy as np

W = [70.1, 91.2, 59.5, 93.2, 53.5]
H = [1.8, 2.0, 1.7, 1.9, 1.6]
np.cov(H, W)
Out[4]:  输出[4]：
array([[  0.025,   2.727],
       [  2.727, 327.235]])

That doesn't agree with our calculation! What went wrong? Nothing. NumPy applies a correction for small sample sizes; it uses 1N−11N−1 as the normalization term instead of 1N1N.
这与我们的计算结果不符！到底哪里出错了？其实没什么问题。NumPy 在处理小样本量时会有相应的修正措施：它使用 1N−11N−1 作为标准化参数，而不是 1N1N 。

This is a bit beyond the scope of this book. Briefly, suppose the actual class size is 200 students, and we took a sample of 5 students to perform this computation because we couldn't afford to measure and weigh all 200 students. It is nearly certain that there will be some error in our estimator because the sample is unlikely to perfectly represent the class. As our sample size approaches 200 the error will approach 0. We say there is no bias in the latter, and that we have an unbiased estimator. In contrast, when we take a small sample there is bias (error is nonzero), and we have a biased estimator.
这超出了本书的讨论范围。简单来说，假设某个班级的实际学生人数为 200 人，但由于无法对所有 200 名学生进行测量，我们只抽取了 5 名学生作为样本来进行计算。可以肯定的是，由于样本无法完全代表整个班级的情况，我们的估算结果必然会存在误差。当样本大小接近 200 时，误差会趋近于 0。此时，我们说该估算结果是无偏的。相反，如果样本规模较小，那么估算结果就会存在偏差，即误差不为零，此时该估算结果就是有偏的。

If the error is zero it makes sense to divide by NN. I will not prove why, but for biased estimators we use 1N−11N−1 to correct for the small sample size. NumPy does this by default because in practice we are almost always working from data samples from a larger collection. If you want the unbiased estimator, which we computed above, use bias=1 in the call to `np.cov'.
如果误差为零，那么用 NN 来除以该数值是合理的。我不打算详细解释原因，但对于有偏差的估计值，我们需要用 1N−11N−1 来修正因样本量过小而导致的误差。NumPy 默认就会这么做，因为在实际应用中，我们处理的几乎都是来自更大数据集的样本。如果你想要上面计算出的无偏估计值，那么在调用`np.cov`时，应使用 bias=1 。

In [5]:  在[5]中：
np.cov(H, W, bias=1)
Out[5]:  输出[5]：
array([[  0.02 ,   2.182],
       [  2.182, 261.788]])

This agrees with our computation. We will not use bias=1 again in this book since we are using random variables which are sampling from the infinite set of positions of the objects we are tracking. Here we are computing the variance and covariance for the entire population, so bias=1 is correct.
这与我们的计算结果一致。在本书中，我们不会再使用 bias=1 了。因为我们所使用的随机变量，其实是从我们所追踪的各个物体的所有可能位置中抽取的。在这里，我们计算的是整个样本群体的方差和协方差，因此 bias=1 的数值是正确的。

What does this matrix tell us? It tells us the variance in heights is 0.02 m2m2 and the variance in weights is 261.788 kg2kg2. Furthermore, it tells us the weights and heights are positively correlated - as heights increase so do the weights.
这个矩阵告诉我们什么信息呢？它表明，身高的方差为 0.02 m2m2 ，而体重的方差则为 261.788 kg2kg2 。此外，该矩阵还显示，体重和身高之间存在正相关关系——也就是说，随着身高的增加，体重也会相应增加。

Let's create perfectly correlated data. By this I mean that the data perfectly fits on a line - there is no variance from the line.
让我们来创建完全相关的数据吧。所谓“完全相关”，指的是数据点完全沿着一条直线分布——没有任何偏离这条直线的现象。

In [6]:  在[6]中：
X = np.linspace(1, 10, 100)
Y = np.linspace(1, 10, 100)
np.cov(X, Y)
Out[6]:  输出[6]：
array([[6.956, 6.956],
       [6.956, 6.956]])

We can see from the covariance matrix that the covariance is equal to the variance in x and in y.
从协方差矩阵可以看出，x 和 y 的协方差实际上等于各自的方差。

Now let's add some noise to one of the variables so that they are no longer perfectly correlated. I will make YY negative to create a negative correlation.
现在，让我们对其中一个变量添加一些“噪声”，这样它们就不再完全相关了。我会把 YY 的值设为负数，从而产生负相关关系。

In [7]:  在[7]中：
X = np.linspace(1, 10, 100)
Y = -(np.linspace(1, 5, 100) + np.sin(X)*.2)
plot_correlated_data(X, Y)
print(np.cov(X, Y))

[[ 6.956 -3.084]
 [-3.084  1.387]]

The data no longer forms a straight line. The covariance is σxy=−3.08σxy=−3.08. It is not close to zero compared to the magnitudes of σ2xσx2 and σ2yσy2, and so we know there is still a high degree of correlation. We can verify this by looking at the chart. The data forms nearly a straight line.
这些数据不再呈直线形态。协方差值为 σxy=−3.08σxy=−3.08 。与 σ2xσx2 和 σ2yσy2 的数值相比，该值远非零，因此可以确定两者之间仍然存在高度相关性。我们可以通过查看图表来验证这一点：数据点大致呈直线分布。

Now I will add random noise to a straight line.
现在，我会在一条直线上添加随机噪声。

In [8]:  在[8]中：
from numpy.random import randn
X = np.linspace(1, 10, 1000) + randn(1000)*2
Y = np.linspace(1, 5, 1000) + randn(1000)
plot_correlated_data(X, Y)
print(np.cov(X, Y))

[[10.295  2.875]
 [ 2.875  2.299]]

We see that the covariance is smaller in relation to the variances, reflecting the lower correlation between XX and YY. We can still fit a straight line through this data, but there is much greater variation in the data.
我们发现，协方差相对于方差来说要小，这反映出 XX 与 YY 之间的相关性较低。我们仍然可以用直线来拟合这些数据，但数据的波动幅度要大得多。

Finally, here is the covariance between completely random data.
最后，这是完全随机数据之间的协方差。

In [9]:  在[9]中：
X = randn(100000)
Y = randn(100000)
plot_correlated_data(X, Y)
print(np.cov(X, Y))

[[1.003 0.003]
 [0.003 1.001]]

Here the covariances are very near zero. As you can see with the plot, there is no clear way to draw a line to fit the data. A vertical line would be as unconvincing as the horizontal line I've shown.
这里的协方差值几乎为零。从图表中可以看出，根本无法画出一条能够准确描述数据的直线。无论是垂直线还是水平线，都同样无法有效描述这些数据。

Multivariate Normal Distribution Equation
多元正态分布方程¶

Recall the equation for the normal distribution from the Gaussians chapter:
请回想一下高斯分布相关章节中关于正态分布的公式：

f(x,μ,σ)=12πσ2−−−−√exp[−12(x−μ)2/σ2]f(x,μ,σ)=12πσ2exp⁡[−12(x−μ)2/σ2]

Here is the multivariate normal distribution in nn dimensions.
以下是 nn 维空间中的多元正态分布。

f(x,μ,Σ)=1(2π)n|Σ|−−−−−−−√exp[−12(x−μ)TΣ−1(x−μ)]f(x,μ,Σ)=1(2π)n|Σ|exp⁡[−12(x−μ)TΣ−1(x−μ)]

The multivariate version merely replaces the scalars of the univariate equations with matrices. If you are reasonably well-versed in linear algebra this equation should look quite manageable. If not, don't worry, both FilterPy and SciPy provide functions to compute it for you. Let's ignore the computation for a moment and plot it to see what it looks like.
多元版本只不过是用矩阵来替代了单变量方程中的标量而已。如果你对线性代数有一定的了解，那么这个方程应该不难理解。如果你不太熟悉线性代数，也不用担心，FilterPy 和 SciPy 都提供了相应的函数来帮你计算。暂且先不考虑具体的计算过程，我们先来绘制一下这个方程的图像，看看它到底是什么样子的。

In [10]:  在[10]中：
import kf_book.mkf_internal as mkf_internal

mean = [2., 17.]
cov = [[10., 0.], 
       [0., 4.]]

mkf_internal.plot_3d_covariance(mean, cov)

This is a plot of multivariate Gaussian with a mean of μ=[217]μ=[217] and a covariance of Σ=[10004]Σ=[10004]. The three dimensional shape shows the probability density for any value of (X,Y)(X,Y) in the z-axis. I have projected the variance for x and y onto the walls of the chart - you can see that they take on the Gaussian bell curve shape. The curve for XX is wider than the curve for YY, which is explained by σ2x=10σx2=10 and σ2y=4σy2=4. The highest point of the 3D surface is at the the means for XX and YY.
这是一幅多元高斯分布的图示。其均值為 μ=[217]μ=[217] ，协方差为 Σ=[10004]Σ=[10004] 。三维图形展示了当 z 轴上的 (X,Y)(X,Y) 取不同值时，相应的概率密度情况。我将 x 轴和 y 轴的方差值投影到了图表的各个面上——可以看到，这些投影都呈现出高斯曲线形状。 XX 对应的曲线比 YY 对应的曲线更宽，这一现象可以用 σ2x=10σx2=10 和 σ2y=4σy2=4 来解释。该三维曲面的最高点位于 XX 和 YY 的均值所在的位置。

All multivariate Gaussians have this shape. If we think of this as the Gaussian for the position of a dog, the z-value at each point of (X,YX,Y) is the probability density of the dog being at that position. Strictly speaking this is the joint probability density function, which I will define soon. So, the dog has the highest probability of being near (2, 17), a modest probability of being near (5, 14), and a very low probability of being near (10, 10). As with the univariate case this is a probability density, not a probability. Continuous distributions have an infinite range, and so the probability of being exactly at (2, 17), or any other point, is 0%. We can compute the probability of being within a given range by computing the volume under the surface with an integral.
所有的多元高斯分布都具有这种形状。如果把这种分布看作是描述狗的位置的模型，那么在每个点上的 z 值就代表了狗处于该位置的概率密度。严格来说，这其实是一个联合概率密度函数，我稍后会详细解释。因此，狗出现在(2, 17)附近的概率最高；出现在(5, 14)附近的概率适中；而出现在(10, 10)附近的概率则非常低。与单变量情况类似，这里描述的是概率密度，而非具体的概率值。连续型分布的范围是无限的，因此，狗恰好出现在(2, 17)或任何其他特定点的概率都是 0%。我们可以通过计算曲面下的积分来得到狗处于某个特定范围内的概率。

FilterPy [2] implements the equation with the function multivariate_gaussian() in the filterpy.stats. module. SciPy's stats module implements the multivariate normal equation with multivariate_normal(). It implements a 'frozen' form where you set the mean and covariance once, and then calculate the probability density for any number of values for x over any arbitrary number of calls. I named my function multivariate_gaussian() to ensure it is never confused with the SciPy version.
FilterPy [2]在 filterpy.stats. 模块中利用 multivariate_gaussian() 函数来实现相关计算。SciPy 的 stats 模块则利用 multivariate_normal() 函数来实现多元正态分布的相关计算。该模块采用了“固定参数”的方式：只需一次性设定均值和协方差，之后就可以对任意数量的 x 值进行概率密度计算，无论调用多少次都是如此。我将自己的函数命名为 multivariate_gaussian() ，以避免与 SciPy 的函数混淆。

The tutorial[1] for the scipy.stats module explains 'freezing' distributions and other very useful features.
scipy.stats 模块的教程[1]详细介绍了如何“冻结”数据分布，以及该模块的其他实用功能。

In [11]:  在[11]中：
from filterpy.stats import gaussian, multivariate_gaussian

I'll demonstrate using it, and then move on to more interesting things.
我会先演示如何使用它，然后再介绍一些更有趣的内容。

First, let's find the probability density for our dog being at (2.5, 7.3) if we believe he is at (2, 7) with a variance of 8 for xx and a variance of 3 for yy.
首先，假设我们的狗位于点(2, 7)，其中 xx 的方差为 8， yy 的方差为 3。那么，我们就可以计算出狗位于点(2.5, 7.3)时的概率密度。

Start by setting xx to (2.5, 7.3). You can use a tuple, list, or NumPy array.
首先，将 xx 设置为(2.5, 7.3)。你可以使用元组、列表或 NumPy 数组来表示这个数值。

In [12]:  在[12]中：
x = [2.5, 7.3]

Next, we set the mean of our belief:
接下来，我们确定信念的均值：

In [13]:  在[13]中：
mu = [2.0, 7.0]

Finally, we have to define our covariance matrix. In the problem statement we did not mention any correlation between xx and yy, and we will assume there is none. This makes sense; a dog can choose to independently wander in either the xx direction or yy direction without affecting the other. I will use the variable name P. Kalman filters use the name PP for the covariance matrix, and we need to become familiar with the conventions.
最后，我们需要确定协方差矩阵的数值。在问题描述中，我们没有提到 xx 和 yy 之间的任何相关性，因此我们假设它们之间没有关联。这是合乎逻辑的：狗可以独立地选择向 xx 方向或 yy 方向移动，而不会影响到另一只狗。我会用 P 来表示这个变量。卡尔曼滤波器则用 PP 来表示协方差矩阵，我们需要熟悉这种命名方式。

In [14]:  在[14]中：
P = [[8., 0.], 
     [0., 3.]]

Now call the function
现在调用该函数吧。

In [15]:  在[15]中：
%precision 4
multivariate_gaussian(x, mu, P)
Out[15]:  Out[15]：
0.0315

We can get the same result from the scipy.stats module.
我们也可以通过 scipy.stats 模块得到同样的结果。

In [16]:  在[16]中：
import scipy
from scipy.stats import multivariate_normal
print(f'{multivariate_normal(mu, P).pdf(x):.4f}')

0.0315

It's time to define some terms. The joint probability, denoted P(x,y)P(x,y), is the probability of both xx and yy happening. For example, if you roll two die P(2,5)P(2,5) is the probability of the first die rolling a 2 and the second die rolling a 5. Assuming the die are six sided and fair, the probability P(2,5)=16×16=136P(2,5)=16×16=136. The 3D chart above shows the joint probability density function.
是时候定义一些术语了。联合概率，用 P(x,y)P(x,y) 表示，指的是 xx 和 yy 同时发生的概率。例如，如果你掷两个骰子， P(2,5)P(2,5) 就是第一个骰子掷出 2、第二个骰子掷出 5 的概率。假设这两个骰子都是六面体且公平无偏，那么该概率为 P(2,5)=16×16=136P(2,5)=16×16=136 。上方的三维图表展示了联合概率密度函数。

The marginal probability is the probability of an event happening without regard of any other event. In the chart above the Gaussian curve drawn to the left is the marginal for YY. This is the probability for the dog being at any position in YY disregarding the value for XX. Earlier I wrote "I have projected the variance for x and y onto the walls of the chart"; these are the marginal probabilities for xx and yy. Another computational benefit of Gaussians is that the marginal of a multivariate Gaussian is another Gaussian!
边际概率是指在不考虑其他任何事件的情况下，某个事件发生的概率。在上图中，左侧的高斯曲线就是 YY 的边际概率。也就是说，这就是狗位于 YY 的任意位置时的概率，而与 XX 的值无关。我之前说过：“我已经把 x 和 y 的方差分别标在了图的各个位置上”；这些其实就是 xx 和 yy 的边际概率。高斯分布的另一个优点是：多元高斯分布的边际分布仍然是一个高斯分布！

Let's look at this in a slightly different way. Instead of plotting a surface showing the probability distribution I will generate 1,000 points with the distribution of [8003][8003].
让我们从稍微不同的角度来考虑这个问题。我不会绘制出表示概率分布的图形，而是会生成 1,000 个点，这些点的分布符合 [8003][8003] 的分布规律。

In [17]:  在[17]中：
mkf_internal.plot_3d_sampled_covariance(mu, P)

We can think of the sampled points as being possible locations for our dog given those particular mean and covariances. The contours on the side show the marginal probability for XX and YY. We can see that he is far more likely to be at (2, 7) where there are many points, than at (-5, 5) where there are few.
我们可以把这些采样点视为：在给定的均值和协方差条件下，狗狗可能出现的位置。旁边的等高线则代表了 XX 和 YY 的边际概率。可以看出，狗狗出现在(2, 7)这个位置的可能性要大得多，因为该位置有大量的采样点；而出现在(-5, 5)这个位置的可能性则小得多，因为该位置只有很少的采样点。

As beautiful as these plots are, it is hard to get useful information from them. For example, it is not easy to tell if XX and YY both have the same variance, and how much they are correlated. In most of the book I'll display Gaussians as contour plots.
虽然这些图表看起来很美观，但很难从中获取有用的信息。例如，很难判断 XX 和 YY 是否具有相同的方差，以及它们之间的相关程度如何。在本书的大部分内容中，我都会用等高线图来表示高斯分布。

The contour plots display the range of values that the multivariate Gaussian takes for a specific standard deviation. This is like taking a horizontal slice out of the 3D plot.
等高线图展示了在特定的标准差下，多元高斯分布所取值的范围。这就好比从三维图中切下水平的一层而已。

These plots show the shape of the slice for 3 standard deviations.
这些图表展示了在 3 个标准差范围内的数据分布情况。

In [18]:  在[18]中：
mkf_internal.plot_3_covariances()

For those of you viewing this online or in Juptyer Notebook on your computer, here is an animation of varying the covariance while holding the variance constant.
对于那些通过在线方式或使用电脑上的 Jupyter Notebook 来观看此内容的人士，这里有一个动画，展示了在方差保持不变的情况下，协方差如何发生变化。

(source: http://git.io/vqxLS)
(来源：http://git.io/vqxLS)

These plots look like circles and ellipses. Indeed, it turns out that any slice through the multivariate Gaussian is an ellipse. Hence, in statistics we do not call these 'contour plots', but either error ellipses or confidence ellipses; the terms are interchangable.
这些图形看起来像圆形或椭圆形。事实上，无论从多变量高斯分布中截取哪个截面，得到的都是一条椭圆形。因此，在统计学中，我们不把这些图形称为“等高线图”，而是称之为“误差椭圆”或“置信椭圆”；这两个术语可以互换使用。

This code uses the function plot_covariance_ellipse() from filterpy.stats. By default the function displays one standard deviation, but you can use either the variance or std parameter to control what is displayed. For example, variance=3**2 or std=3 would display the 3rd standard deviation, and variance=[1,4,9] or std=[1,2,3] would display the 1st, 2nd, and 3rd standard deviations.
该代码使用了 filterpy.stats 中的 plot_covariance_ellipse() 函数。默认情况下，该函数会显示一个标准差数值。不过，你也可以使用 variance 或 std 参数来指定要显示的内容。例如，使用 variance=3**2 或 std=3 可以显示第三个标准差；而使用 variance=[1,4,9] 或 std=[1,2,3] 则可以同时显示第一个、第二个和第三个标准差。

In [19]:  在[19]中：
from filterpy.stats import plot_covariance_ellipse
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

P = [[2, 0], [0, 6]]
plot_covariance_ellipse((2, 7), P, fc='g', alpha=0.2, 
                        std=[1, 2, 3],
                        title='|2 0|\n|0 6|')
plt.gca().grid(b=False);

The solid colors may suggest to you that the probability distribution is constant between the standard deviations. This is not true, as you can tell from the 3D plot of the Gaussian. Here is a 2D shaded representation of the probability distribution for the covariance (21.21.21.3)21.21.21.3). Darker gray corresponds to higher probability density.
这些纯色区域可能会让你误以为，概率分布在整个标准差范围内是恒定的。但实际上并非如此，从高斯函数的 3D 图上就可以看出来。下图是协方差概率分布的 2D 示意图：颜色越深，表示概率密度越高。

In [20]:  在[20]中：
from kf_book.nonlinear_plots import plot_cov_ellipse_colormap
plot_cov_ellipse_colormap(cov=[[2, 1.2], [1.2, 1.3]]);

Thinking about the physical interpretation of these plots clarifies their meaning. The mean and covariance of the first plot is
仔细思考这些图表的物理意义后，就能更清楚地理解它们的含义了。第一个图表的均值和协方差分别为……

μ=[27],Σ=[2002]μ=[27],Σ=[2002]

In [21]:  在[21]中：
x = [2, 7]
P = [[2, 0], [0, 2]]
plot_covariance_ellipse(x, P, fc='g', alpha=0.2, 
                       title='|2 0|\n|0 2|')
plt.gca().grid(b=False)

A Bayesian way of thinking about this is that the ellipse shows us the amount of error in our belief. A tiny circle would indicate that we have a very small error, and a very large circle indicates a lot of error in our belief. The shape of the ellipse shows us the geometric relationship of the errors in XX and YY. Here we have a circle so errors in XX and YY are equally likely.
从贝叶斯的角度来看，椭圆的形状反映了我们信念中的误差大小。如果椭圆非常接近圆形，那就意味着我们的信念中的误差很小；而如果椭圆很扁，那就意味着我们的信念中存在较大的误差。椭圆的形状还体现了 XX 和 YY 之间的误差比例关系。在这个例子中，由于椭圆接近圆形，因此 XX 和 YY 的误差概率是相等的。

The mean and covariance of the second plot are
第二个图形的均值和协方差分别为：

μ=[27],Σ=[2006]μ=[27],Σ=[2006]

In [22]:  在[22]中：
x = [2, 7]
P = [[2, 0], [0, 6]]
plot_covariance_ellipse(x, P, fc='g', alpha=0.2, 
                    title='|2 0|\n|0 6|')
plt.gca().grid(b=False)

This time we use a different variance for XX (σ2x=2σx2=2) vs YY (σ2y=6σy2=6). The result is a tall and narrow ellipse. We can see that a lot more uncertainty in YY vs XX. In both cases we believe the dog is at (2, 7), but the uncertainties are different.
这一次，我们对 XX ( σ2x=2σx2=2 )和 YY ( σ2y=6σy2=6 )使用了不同的方差值。结果得到的是一个又高又窄的椭圆形。可以看出， YY 的不确定性要远远大于 XX 的不确定性。在这两种情况下，我们都认为狗的位置在(2, 7)，但两者之间的不确定性程度不同。

The third plot shows the mean and covariance
第三幅图表展示了平均值和协方差。

μ=[27],Σ=[21.21.22]μ=[27],Σ=[21.21.22]

In [23]:  在[23]中：
x = [2, 7]
P = [[2, 1.2], [1.2, 2]]
plot_covariance_ellipse(x, P, fc='g', alpha=0.2, 
                    title='|2 1.2|\n|1.2 2|')

This is the first contour that has values in the off-diagonal elements of the covariance, and this is the first contour plot with a slanted ellipse. This is not a coincidence. The two facts are telling us the same thing. A slanted ellipse tells us that the xx and yy values are somehow correlated. The off-diagonal elements in the covariance matrix are non-zero, indicating that a correlation exists.
这是第一个在协方差矩阵的非对角线元素上也有数值的等高线图。同时，这也是第一个包含倾斜椭圆的等高线图。这并非巧合。这两个现象其实都在告诉我们同样的道理：倾斜的椭圆表明， xx 和 yy 这两个数值之间存在某种关联。协方差矩阵中的非对角线元素不为零，这也进一步说明了两者之间存在相关性。

Recall the plot for height versus weight. It formed a slanted grouping of points. We can use NumPy's cov() function to compute the covariance of two or more variables by placing them into a 2D array. Let's do that, then plot the 2σ2σ covariance ellipse on top of the data. We will need to use bias=1 because the data represents the entire population; it is not a sample.
回想一下关于身高与体重的散点图。那些点呈现出一种倾斜的分布态势。我们可以利用 NumPy 的函数来计算两个或多个变量之间的协方差，具体方法是把这些变量放入一个二维数组中。完成后，我们可以在数据上方绘制出表示协方差的椭圆。由于这些数据代表了整个总体，而非样本数据，因此我们需要使用相应的函数来进行计算。

In [24]:  在[24]中：
cov_hw = np.cov(np.vstack((height, weight)), bias=1)
cov_hw
Out[24]:  Out[24]：
array([[  18.5249,  135.701 ],
       [ 135.701 , 1092.29  ]])
In [25]:  在[25]中：
plt.scatter(height, weight, s=120, marker='s')
plt.title('Track Team Height vs. Weight')
plt.xlabel('Height (in)'); plt.ylabel('Weight (lbs)')
plot_covariance_ellipse((np.mean(height), np.mean(weight)), cov_hw, fc='g', 
                        alpha=0.2, axis_equal=False, std=2)

This should help you form a strong intuition on the meaning and use of covariances. The covariance ellipse shows you how the data is 'scattered' in relation to each other. A narrow ellipse like this tells you that the data is very correlated. There is only a narrow range of weights for any given height. The ellipse leans towards the right, telling us there is a positive correlation - as x increases y also increases. If the ellipse leaned towards the left then the correlation would be negative - as x increases y decreases. We can see this in the following plot:
这应该有助于你更好地理解协方差的含义和用途。协方差椭圆图能够显示数据之间的相互分布情况。如果椭圆很窄，那就说明数据之间的相关性很强。对于任何给定的身高值，其对应的体重值范围都很窄。如果椭圆向右倾斜，那就说明存在正相关关系——即随着 x 的增加，y 也会增加。如果椭圆向左倾斜，则说明存在负相关关系——即随着 x 的增加，y 会减少。我们可以在下图中看到这一点：

In [26]:  在[26]中：
max_temp = [200, 250, 300, 400, 450, 500]
lifespan = [10, 9.7, 5, 5.4, 4.3, 0.3]

plt.scatter(max_temp, lifespan, s=80)
cov = np.cov(np.vstack((max_temp, lifespan)))
plot_covariance_ellipse((np.mean(max_temp), np.mean(lifespan)), cov, fc='g', 
            alpha=0.2, axis_equal=False, std=2)
plt.title('Engine Temperature vs Lifespan')
plt.xlabel('Temperature (C)'); plt.ylabel('Years');

The relationships between variances and covariances can be hard to puzzle out by inspection, so here is an interactive plot. (If you are reading this in a static form instructions to run this online are here: https://git.io/vza7b)
通过直观观察，很难理解方差和协方差之间的关系。因此，这里有一个交互式图表来帮助理解。如果您是以静态文本形式阅读此内容，请点击此处了解如何在线查看该图表：https://git.io/vza7b

In [27]:  在[27]中：
from ipywidgets import interact
from kf_book.book_plots import figsize, FloatSlider

fig = None
def plot_covariance(var_x, var_y, cov_xy):
    global fig
    if fig: plt.close(fig)
    fig = plt.figure(figsize=(4,4))
    P1 = [[var_x, cov_xy], [cov_xy, var_y]]

    plot_covariance_ellipse((10, 10), P1, axis_equal=False,
                            show_semiaxis=True)

    plt.xlim(4, 16)
    plt.gca().set_aspect('equal')
    plt.ylim(4, 16)

    
with figsize(y=6):
    interact (plot_covariance,           
          var_x=FloatSlider(5, min=0, max=20), 
          var_y=FloatSlider(5, min=0, max=20), 
          cov_xy=FloatSlider(1.5, min=0, max=50, step=.2));

interactive(children=(FloatSlider(value=5.0, continuous_update=False, description='var_x', max=20.0), FloatSli…

Pearson's Correlation Coefficient
皮尔逊相关系数¶

We will not be using this coefficient in this book, but you may see it elsewhere. You can safely skip this section if uninterested.
在本书中，我们不会使用这个系数。不过，你可能会在其他地方看到它。如果你不感兴趣的话，可以直接跳过这一部分。

The correlation between two variables can be given a numerical value with Pearson's Correlation Coefficient. It is defined as
两个变量之间的相关性可以用皮尔逊相关系数来表示为一个数值。其定义如下：

ρxy=COV(X,Y)σxσyρxy=COV(X,Y)σxσy

This value can range in value from -1 to 1. If the covariance is 0 than ρ=0ρ=0. A value greater than 0 indicates that the relationship is a positive correlation, and a negative value indicates that there is a negative correlation. Values near -1 or 1 indicate a very strong correlation, and values near 0 indicate a very weak correlation.
该数值的取值范围在-1 到 1 之间。如果协方差为 0，则表示两者之间没有相关性。数值大于 0 表示正相关，数值小于 0 则表示负相关。接近-1 或 1 的数值表示相关性很强，而接近 0 的数值则表示相关性很弱。

Correlation and covariance are very closely related. Covariance has units associated with it, and correlation is a unitless ratio. For example, for our dog σxyσxy has units of meters squared.
相关性和协方差之间有着密切的联系。协方差具有具体的单位，而相关性则是一个没有单位的比率。例如，对于我们的狗 σxyσxy 来说，其相关性的计算结果单位是平方米。

We can use scipy.stats.pearsonr function to compute the Pearson coefficient. It returns a tuple of the Pearson coefficient and of the 2 tailed p-value. The latter is not used in this book. Here we compute ρρ for height vs weight of student athletes:
我们可以使用 scipy.stats.pearsonr 函数来计算皮尔逊系数。该函数会返回一个包含皮尔逊系数和双尾 p 值的元组。不过，在本书中，我们不会使用双尾 p 值。这里，我们以学生运动员的身高与体重为例来计算 ρρ 值：

In [28]:  在[28]中：
from scipy.stats import pearsonr
pearsonr(height, weight)[0]
Out[28]:  Out[28]：
0.9539731096080194

Here we compute the correlation between engine temperature and lifespan.
在这里，我们计算了发动机温度与使用寿命之间的相关性。

In [29]:  在[29]中：
pearsonr(max_temp, lifespan)[0]
Out[29]:  Out[29]：
-0.9178223453527254

Using Correlations to Improve Estimates
利用相关性来提高估算精度¶

Suppose we believe our dog is at position (5, 10) with some given covariance. If the standard deviation in x and y is each 2 meters, but they are strongly correlated, the covariance contour would look something like this.
假设我们认为，我们的狗位于坐标点(5, 10)处，且该位置的协方差值已知。如果 x 轴和 y 轴上的标准差均为 2 米，但两者之间存在很强的相关性，那么协方差等高线就会呈现类似这样的形状。

In [30]:  在[30]中：
P = [[4, 3.9], [3.9, 4]]

plot_covariance_ellipse((5, 10), P, ec='k', std=[1, 2, 3])
plt.xlabel('X')
plt.ylabel('Y');

Now suppose I were to tell you that we know that x=7.5x=7.5. What can we infer about the value for yy? The position is extremely likely to lie within the 3σσ covariance ellipse. We can infer the position in y based on the covariance matrix because there is a correlation between x and y. I've illustrated the likely range of values for y as a blue filled circle.
现在，假设我告诉您，我们知道 x=7.5x=7.5 的值。那么，关于 yy 的值，我们能推断出什么呢？ yy 的值极有可能位于 3 个 σσ 所构成的协方差椭圆范围内。由于 x 和 y 之间存在相关性，我们可以利用协方差矩阵来推断 y 的值。我用蓝色实心圆标出了 y 的可能取值范围。

In [31]:  在[31]中：
mkf_internal.plot_correlation_covariance()

The circle not mathematically correct, but it gets the idea across. We will tackle the mathematics in the next section. For now recognize that we can predict that yy is likely near 12. A value of y=−10y=−10 is extremely improbable.
这个圆在数学上并不完全准确，但大致能表达出我们的意图。我们将在下一节中详细探讨其中的数学问题。目前可以确定的是， yy 的值很可能接近 12。而 y=−10y=−10 的值则几乎不可能出现。

A word about correlation and independence. If variables are independent they can vary separately. If you walk in an open field, you can move in the xx direction (east-west), the yy direction(north-south), or any combination thereof. Independent variables are always also uncorrelated. Except in special cases, the reverse does not hold true. Variables can be uncorrelated, but dependent. For example, consider y=x2y=x2. Correlation is a linear measurement, so xx and yy are uncorrelated. However, yy is dependent on xx.
关于相关性和独立性需要说明的是：如果各个变量是独立的，那么它们可以各自独立地变化。比如，当你在开阔的田野中行走时，你可以朝东西方向移动，也可以朝南北方向移动，或者以任何组合的方式移动。独立的变量之间必然也是不相关的。不过，特殊情况除外，反之则不成立。变量之间可能不相关，但却有可能是相互依赖的。例如，以 y=x2y=x2 为例。相关性是一种线性度量，因此 xx 和 yy 之间是不相关的。但是， yy 却依赖于 xx 。

Multiplying Multidimensional Gaussians
多维高斯函数的乘法运算¶

In the previous chapter we incorporated an uncertain measurement with an uncertain estimate by multiplying their Gaussians together. The result was another Gaussian with a smaller variance. If two pieces of uncertain information corroborate each other we should be more certain in our conclusion. The graphs look like this:
在上一章中，我们通过将具有不确定性的测量结果和估计值的高斯分布相乘，来处理这些不确定性。这样得到的结果仍然是高斯分布，只不过其方差更小了。如果两段不确定的信息能够相互印证，那么我们得出的结论就会更可靠。相关图表如下所示：

In [32]:  在[32]中：
mkf_internal.plot_gaussian_multiply()

The combination of measurements 1 and 2 yields more certainty, so the new Gaussian is taller and narrower - the variance became smaller. The same happens in multiple dimensions with multivariate Gaussians.
将测量结果 1 和 2 结合起来后，结果的确定性会更高。因此，新的高斯分布曲线会更高、更窄——方差也变小了。在多维情况下，多元高斯分布也是如此。

Here are the equations for multiplying multivariate Gaussians. The capital sigma (ΣΣ) indicates that these are matrices, not scalars. Specifically, they are covariance matrices:
以下是多元高斯分布相乘时的相关公式。大写的西格玛符号表示这些是矩阵，而非标量。具体来说，它们就是协方差矩阵。

μΣ=Σ2(Σ1+Σ2)−1μ1+Σ1(Σ1+Σ2)−1μ2=Σ1(Σ1+Σ2)−1Σ2μ=Σ2(Σ1+Σ2)−1μ1+Σ1(Σ1+Σ2)−1μ2Σ=Σ1(Σ1+Σ2)−1Σ2

They are generated by plugging the multivariate Gaussians for the prior and the estimate into Bayes Theorem. I gave you the algebra for the univariate case in the Gaussians chapter.
它们是通过将先验分布和估计值所对应的多元高斯分布代入贝叶斯定理中计算得出的。在关于高斯分布的那一章中，我已经给出了单变量情况下的数学推导过程。

You will not need to remember these equations as they are computed by Kalman filter equations that will be presented shortly. This computation is also available in FilterPy using the multivariate_multiply() method, which you can import from filterpy.stats.
你无需记住这些方程式，因为它们会由卡尔曼滤波公式来计算，这些公式稍后会详细介绍。在 FilterPy 中，也可以使用 multivariate_multiply() 方法来进行这些计算，你可以通过 filterpy.stats 来导入该函数。

To give you some intuition about this, recall the equations for multiplying univariate Gaussians:
为了让你更直观地理解这一点，不妨回想一下关于单变量高斯分布相乘的公式：

μσ2=σ21μ2+σ22μ1σ21+σ22,=σ21σ22σ21+σ22μ=σ12μ2+σ22μ1σ12+σ22,σ2=σ12σ22σ12+σ22

This looks similar to the equations for the multivariate equations. This will be more obvious if you recognize that matrix inversion, denoted by the -1 power, is like a reciprocal since AA−1=IAA−1=I. I will rewrite the inversions as divisions - this is not a mathematically correct thing to do as division for matrices is not defined, but it does help us compare the equations.
这看起来与多元方程的公式很相似。如果你明白矩阵求逆运算——用-1 的幂来表示——其实相当于求倒数的话，那么这一点就更加明显了。我会把矩阵求逆运算改写为除法形式。不过从数学上来说，这种做法并不完全正确，因为矩阵的除法并没有明确定义。不过，这样做有助于我们更好地理解这些方程式。

μΣ≈Σ2μ1+Σ1μ2Σ1+Σ2≈Σ1Σ2(Σ1+Σ2)μ≈Σ2μ1+Σ1μ2Σ1+Σ2Σ≈Σ1Σ2(Σ1+Σ2)

In this form the relationship between the univariate and multivariate equations is clear.
在这种形式下，单变量方程与多变量方程之间的关系就一目了然了。

Now let's explore multivariate Gaussians in terms of a concrete example. Suppose that we are tracking an aircraft with two radar systems. I will ignore altitude so I can use two dimensional plots. Radar provides the range and bearing to a target. We start out being uncertain about the position of the aircraft, so the covariance, which is our uncertainty about the position, might look like this. In the language of Bayesian statistics this is our prior.
现在，让我们通过一个具体的例子来了解多元高斯分布。假设我们使用两个雷达系统来追踪一架飞机的位置。为了简化问题，我们不考虑飞机的飞行高度，因此可以使用二维坐标图来表示飞机的位置。雷达可以提供目标到探测器的距离和方位角。由于我们最初并不清楚飞机的确切位置，因此表示位置不确定性的协方差可能如下所示。在贝叶斯统计学中，这被称为“先验概率”。

In [33]:  在[33]中：
P0 = [[6, 0], [0, 6]]
plot_covariance_ellipse((10, 10), P0, fc='y', alpha=0.6)

Now suppose that there is a radar to the lower left of the aircraft. Further suppose that the radar's bearing measurement is accurate, but the range measurement is inaccurate. The covariance for the error in the measurement might look like this (plotted in green on top of the yellow prior):
现在，假设在飞机的左下方有一个雷达。进一步假设，该雷达的方位测量是准确的，但距离测量则不准确。那么，这些测量误差的协方差可能如下所示（以绿色线条显示在黄色的基准线上方）：

In [34]:  在[34]中：
P1 = [[2, 1.9], [1.9, 2]]
plot_covariance_ellipse((10, 10), P0, fc='y', alpha=0.6)
plot_covariance_ellipse((10, 10), P1, fc='g', alpha=0.9)

Recall that Bayesian statistics calls this the evidence. The ellipse points towards the radar. It is very long because the range measurement is inaccurate, and the aircraft could be within a considerable distance of the measured range. It is very narrow because the bearing estimate is very accurate and thus the aircraft must be very close to the bearing estimate.
请记住，贝叶斯统计学将此称为“证据”。椭圆的中心指向雷达所探测到的位置。由于测距不准确，椭圆的长度相当大；实际上，飞机可能与测得的距离有相当大的偏差。不过，由于方位角的测量非常精确，因此椭圆的宽度很小，这意味着飞机很可能就在该方位角所指示的位置附近。

We want to find the posterior - the mean and covariance that results from incorporating the evidence into the prior. As in every other chapter we combine evidence by multiplying them together.
我们希望找到后验概率——也就是在将各种证据纳入先验概率之后所得到的均值和协方差。与其他章节的做法一样，我们通过将各种证据相乘来合并它们。

In [35]:  在[35]中：
from filterpy.stats import multivariate_multiply

P2 = multivariate_multiply((10, 10), P0, (10, 10), P1)[1]
plot_covariance_ellipse((10, 10), P0, ec='k', fc='y', alpha=0.2)
plot_covariance_ellipse((10, 10), P1, ec='k', fc='g', alpha=0.9)
plot_covariance_ellipse((10, 10), P2, ec='k', fc='b')

I have plotted the original estimate (prior) in a very transparent yellow, the radar reading in green (evidence), and the finale estimate (posterior) in blue.
我用透明的黄色标出了最初的估算值（先验值），用绿色标出了雷达测得的数值（证据），再用蓝色标出了最终的估算值（后验值）。

The posterior retained the same shape and position as the radar measurement, but is smaller. We've seen this with one dimensional Gaussians. Multiplying two Gaussians makes the variance smaller because we are incorporating more information, hence we are less uncertain. Another point to recognize is that the covariance shape reflects the physical layout of the aircraft and the radar system. The importance of this will become clear in the next step.
后部的形状和位置与雷达测量结果一致，不过尺寸要小一些。我们在一维高斯分布中也观察到了这种现象。当将两个高斯分布相乘时，方差会减小，因为这样处理能整合更多信息，从而降低不确定性。另外需要注意的是，协方差的形状反映了飞机与雷达系统的物理布局。这一点在接下来的步骤中会变得更加清晰。

Now let's say we get a measurement from a second radar, this one to the lower right. The posterior from the last step becomes our new prior, which I plot in yellow. The new measurement is plotted in green.
现在，假设我们从另一个雷达那里获得了测量数据，也就是右下角的那个雷达。上一步得到的后验概率值就变成了新的先验概率值，我用黄色来表示这个新的先验概率值。新的测量数据则用绿色来表示。

In [36]:  在[36]中：
P3 = [[2, -1.9], [-1.9, 2.2]]
plot_covariance_ellipse((10, 10), P2, ec='k', fc='y', alpha=0.6)
plot_covariance_ellipse((10, 10), P3, ec='k', fc='g', alpha=0.6)

We incorporate this information by multiplying the Gaussians:
我们通过将高斯函数相乘来整合这些信息：

In [37]:  在[37]中：
P4 = multivariate_multiply((10, 10), P2, (10, 10), P3)[1]
plot_covariance_ellipse((10, 10), P2, ec='k', fc='y', alpha=0.6)
plot_covariance_ellipse((10, 10), P3, ec='k', fc='g', alpha=0.6)
plot_covariance_ellipse((10, 10), P4, ec='k', fc='b')

The only likely place for the aircraft is where the two ellipses intersect. The intersection, formed by multiplying the prior and measurement, is a new Gaussian. The shapes reflects the geometry of the problem. This allows us to triangulate on the aircraft, resulting in a very accurate estimate. We didn't explicitly write any code to perform triangulation; it was a natural outcome of multiplying the Gaussians of each measurement together.
飞机最可能所在的位置，就是那两个椭圆的交点处。这个交点是通过将先前的数据与测量结果相乘而得到的，其结果是一个新的高斯分布。这些几何形状反映了问题的本质特征。这样一来，我们就可以对飞机的位置进行精确的估算。实际上，我们并没有专门编写代码来进行三角测量；这只是将各项测量结果对应的高斯分布相乘后的自然结果而已。

Think back to the g-h Filter chapter where we displayed the error bars of two weighings on a scale. The estimate must fall somewhere within the region where the error bars overlap. Here the estimate must fall between 161 to 163 pounds.
回想一下关于 g-h 滤波器的那一章内容：我们当时展示了用秤称量两次所得结果的误差范围。估计值应该位于这两个误差范围所覆盖的区域内。在这里，估计值应该在 161 磅到 163 磅之间。

In [38]:  在[38]中：
import kf_book.book_plots as book_plots
book_plots.plot_errorbars([(160, 8, 'A'), (170, 8, 'B')], xlims=(150, 180))

Let's consider a different layout. Suppose the first radar is directly to the left of the aircraft. I can model the measurement error with
让我们考虑另一种布局。假设第一台雷达位于飞机的正左侧。我可以用某种方式来模拟这种测量误差。

Σ=[2000.2]Σ=[2000.2]

Here we see the result of multiplying the prior with the measurement.
这里我们看到了将先验概率与测量结果相乘后的结果。

In [39]:  在[39]中：
P1 = [[2, 0], [0, .2]]
P2 = multivariate_multiply((10, 10), P0, (10, 10), P1)[1]
plot_covariance_ellipse((10, 10), P0, ec='k', fc='y', alpha=0.2)
plot_covariance_ellipse((10, 10), P1, ec='k', fc='g', alpha=0.6)
plot_covariance_ellipse((10, 10), P2, ec='k', fc='b')

Now we can incorporate the measurement from the second radar system, which we will leave in the same position as before.
现在，我们可以把第二个雷达系统的测量数据也纳入考虑范围了。该雷达系统仍保持与之前相同的位置。

In [40]:  在[40]中：
P3 = [[2, -1.9], [-1.9, 2.2]]
P4 = multivariate_multiply((10, 10), P2, (10, 10), P3)[1]
plot_covariance_ellipse((10, 10), P2, ec='k', fc='y', alpha=0.2)
plot_covariance_ellipse((10, 10), P3, ec='k', fc='g', alpha=0.6)
plot_covariance_ellipse((10, 10), P4, ec='k', fc='b')

Our estimate is not as accurate as the previous example. The two radar stations are no longer orthogonal to each other relative to the aircraft's position so the triangulation is not optimal.
我们的估算结果不如前面的例子准确。由于这两个雷达站相对于飞机的位置不再处于正交状态，因此三角测量结果的准确性也就大打折扣了。

For a final example, imagine taking two measurements from the same radar a short time apart. The covariance ellipses will nearly overlap, leaving a very large error in our new estimate:
最后一个例子：假设我们在短时间内从同一台雷达设备上获取了两次测量数据。此时，两个协方差椭圆几乎会重叠在一起，从而导致我们的新估算结果出现很大的误差。

In [41]:  在[41]中：
P5 = multivariate_multiply((10,10), P2, (10.1, 9.97), P2)
plot_covariance_ellipse((10, 10), P2, ec='k', fc='y', alpha=0.2)
plot_covariance_ellipse((10.1, 9.97), P2, ec='k', fc='g', alpha=0.6)
plot_covariance_ellipse(P5[0], P5[1], ec='k', fc='b')
plt.xlim(6, 14);

Hidden Variables  隐藏变量¶

You can already see why a multivariate Kalman filter can perform better than a univariate one. Correlations between variables can significantly improve our estimates. We can take this much further. This section contains the key insight to this chapter, so read carefully.
你现在应该明白，为什么多元卡尔曼滤波器的性能会优于单变量卡尔曼滤波器了。各变量之间的相关性能够显著提升我们的估算精度。我们还可以进一步探讨这个问题。本节包含了本章的核心思想，请仔细阅读。

Let's say we are tracking an aircraft and we get the following data for the xx and yy coordinates at time tt=1, 2, and 3 seconds. What does your intuition tell you the value of xx will be at time tt=4 seconds?
假设我们正在追踪一架飞机，我们在时间点 tt 为 1 秒、2 秒和 3 秒时，分别得到了 xx 和 yy 坐标的数据。那么，凭直觉来看，当时间点 tt 为 4 秒时， xx 的数值会是多少呢？

In [42]:  在[42]中：
mkf_internal.show_position_chart()

It appears that the aircraft is flying in a straight line and we know that aircraft cannot turn on a dime. The most reasonable guess is that at tt=4 the aircraft is at (4,4). I will depict that with a green arrow.
看起来，该飞机是沿直线飞行的。我们知道，飞机不可能瞬间改变飞行方向。最合理的推测是：在 tt =4 时，飞机的位置应该在(4,4)处。我会用绿色箭头来标示这个位置。

In [43]:  在[43]中：
mkf_internal.show_position_prediction_chart()

You made this inference because you inferred a constant velocity for the airplane. The reasonable assumption is that the aircraft is moving one unit each in x and y per time step.
你得出这个结论，是因为你假设飞机的速度是恒定的。合理的假设应该是：在每个时间单位内，飞机在 x 轴和 y 轴上各移动一个单位长度。

Think back to the g-h Filter chapter when we were trying to improve the weight predictions of a noisy scale. We incorporated weight gain into the equations because it allowed us to make a better prediction of the weight the next day. The g-h filter uses the gg parameter to scale the amount of significance given to the current weight measurement, and the hh parameter scaled the amount of significance given to the weight gain.
回想一下在 g-h 滤波器那一章的内容吧。当时，我们试图改进那些受噪声干扰的体重测量结果。我们将体重变化纳入了计算公式中，因为这样能更准确地预测第二天的体重数值。g-h 滤波器利用 gg 参数来调整对当前体重测量的重视程度，而 hh 参数则用于调整对体重变化的重视程度。

We are going to do the same thing with our Kalman filter. After all, the Kalman filter is a form of a g-h filter. In this case we are tracking an airplane, so instead of weight and weight gain we need to track position and velocity. Weight gain is the derivative of weight, and of course velocity is the derivative of position. It's impossible to plot and understand the 4D chart that would be needed to plot x and y and their respective velocities so let's do it for xx, knowing that the math generalizes to more dimensions.
我们对卡尔曼滤波器也会采用同样的处理方式。毕竟，卡尔曼滤波器本质上也是一种 G-H 滤波器。在这里，我们需要追踪的是飞机的位置和速度，而不是重量或重量的变化。重量的变化其实就是重量的导数，而速度自然就是位置的导数。要绘制出表示 x、y 以及它们对应速度的 4 维图表是不可能的，因此我们只需针对 xx 的情况来处理即可。当然，这种数学原理同样适用于更多维度的情况。

At time 1 we might be fairly certain about the position (x=0) but have no idea about the velocity. We can plot that with a covariance matrix like this. The narrower width expresses our relative certainty about position, and the tall height expresses our lack of knowledge about velocity.
在时间点 1，我们或许能够相当确定物体的位置（x=0），但完全不知道其速度。我们可以用协方差矩阵来表示这一情况。矩阵的宽度越窄，说明我们对物体位置的确定性越高；而矩阵的高度则反映了我们对物体速度的了解程度有多低。

In [44]:  在[44]中：
mkf_internal.show_x_error_chart(1)

However, position and velocity are correlated. If the velocity is 5 m/s, then in 1 second the position will be 5 m. If the velocity is -10 m/s then in 1 second the position will be -10 m. Let's visualize this with a velocity covariance drawn on the diagonal.
不过，位置和速度是相互关联的。如果速度为 5 米/秒，那么 1 秒钟后，位置就是 5 米。如果速度为-10 米/秒，那么 1 秒钟后，位置就是-10 米。我们可以通过在坐标系中对角线上绘制速度相关图来直观地理解这一点。

In [45]:  在[45]中：
mkf_internal.show_x_error_chart(2)

At this point the velocity covariance doesn't help us. We can't predict a new position because we have no idea what the velocity is. But after one second we get a position update of x=5.
此时，速度的协方差对我们没有任何帮助。因为我们不知道物体的速度是多少，所以无法预测其新的位置。不过，一秒钟后，我们得到了新的位置信息：x=5。

In [46]:  在[46]中：
mkf_internal.show_x_error_chart(3)

It won't be clear until the next chapter how I calculate what I'm about to say. Ignore the calculation, and think about what this implies. We have no easy way to say where the object really is because we are so uncertain about the velocity. Hence the ellipse stretches very far in the x-axis. Our uncertainty in velocity of course means it is also very spread in the y-axis. But as I said in the last paragraph, position is correlated to velocity. If the velocity is 5 m/s the next position would be 5, and if the velocity is 10 the next position would be 10. They are very correlated, so the ellipse must be very narrow.
直到下一章，我们才能明白我究竟是如何计算出上述结果的。先不必考虑具体的计算过程，只需思考其背后的含义。由于我们对物体速度的测量存在很大不确定性，因此我们无法准确确定物体的实际位置。正因如此，椭圆在 x 轴方向上会延伸得很远。当然，由于速度的不确定性，椭圆在 y 轴方向上也会分布得很广。但正如我在上一段中所说的，物体的位置与速度是相关的。如果速度为 5 米/秒，那么下一个位置就是 5；如果速度为 10 米/秒，下一个位置就是 10。由于两者高度相关，因此椭圆在 y 轴方向上应该相当狭窄。

This superposition of the two covariances is where the magic happens. The only reasonable estimate at time t=1 (where position=5) is roughly the intersection between the velocity covariance and measurement covariance! More exactly, we can use the math from the last section and multiply the two covariances together. From a Bayesian point of view we multiply the prior with the probability of the evidence (the likelihood) to get the posterior. If we multiply the position covariance with the velocity covariance using the Bayesian equations we get this result:
这两种协方差的叠加效应，正是产生“神奇效果”的关键所在。在 t=1 时刻（此时位置为 5），最合理的估计值大致就是速度协方差与测量协方差的乘积！更准确地说，我们可以运用上一节中的数学公式，将这两个协方差相乘。从贝叶斯的角度来看，我们需要将先验概率与证据的概率相乘，从而得到后验概率。如果我们按照贝叶斯公式将位置协方差与速度协方差相乘，就能得到这样的结果：

In [47]:  在[47]中：
mkf_internal.show_x_error_chart(4)

The new covariance (the posterior) lies at the intersection of the position covariance and the velocity covariance. It is slightly tilted, showing that there is some correlation between the position and velocity. Far more importantly, it is much smaller than either the position or velocity covariances. In the previous chapter our variance would get smaller each time we performed an update() because the previous estimate was multiplied by the new measurement. The same happens here. However, here the improvement is markedly better. This is because we are using two different pieces of information which are nevertheless correlated. Knowing the velocity approximately and their correlation and the position approximately allows us to make a very accurate estimate. It is not easy to see in this diagram, but the uncertainty in position has become smaller. We know this because the new covariance doesn't reach as far in the x-axis as the measurement covariance at t=1. So we are not just more certain about velocity, but we are also more certain about the position than we would be if we only used position measurement without considering the velocity!
新的协方差值位于位置协方差和速度协方差的交点上。该值略微倾斜，表明位置和速度之间存在一定的相关性。更重要的是，这个新协方差值远远小于位置协方差和速度协方差中的任何一个。在上一章中，每次进行测量后，方差都会变小，因为之前的估计值会与新的测量结果相乘。这里的情况也是如此。不过，这里的改进效果更为显著。这是因为我们利用了两个相互关联的信息源。只要能大致了解速度及其与位置的相关性，就能得出非常准确的估计值。从图中不太容易看出来，但位置的不确定性确实变小了。我们可以确定这一点，因为新的协方差值在 x 轴上的范围比单独的测量结果要小。在 t=1 时刻的协方差值。这样一来，我们不仅对速度的测量更准确，对位置的测量也更为精确。如果只考虑位置信息而不考虑速度的话，所得到的结果当然会不那么准确。

This is a key point, so read carefully! The radar is only detecting the position of the aircraft. This is called an observed variable. Based on the position estimates we can compute velocity. We call the velocity a hidden variable. Hidden means what it sounds like - there is no sensor that is measuring velocity, thus its value is hidden from us. We are able to use the correlation between position and velocity to infer its value very accurately.
这是一个关键点，请仔细阅读！雷达只能检测到飞机的位置。这一数值被称为“可观测变量”。根据这些位置数据，我们可以计算出飞机的速度。这个速度则被称为“隐藏变量”。所谓“隐藏变量”，顾名思义，就是没有专门的传感器来测量这个数值，因此我们无法直接得知它的大小。不过，我们可以利用位置和速度之间的关联关系，非常精确地推断出速度的值。

To round out the terminology there are also unobserved variables. For example, the aircraft's state includes things such as as heading, engine RPM, weight, color, the first name of the pilot, and so on. We cannot sense these directly using the position sensor so they are not observed. There is no way to infer them from the sensor measurements and correlations (red planes don't go faster than white planes), so they are not hidden. Instead, they are unobservable. If you include an unobserved variable in your filter state the estimate for that variable will be nonsense.
再补充一点术语说明：还有那些无法被直接观测到的变量。例如，飞机的各种状态参数包括飞行方向、发动机转速、重量、颜色、飞行员的姓名等等。我们无法通过位置传感器直接获取这些信息，因此它们属于无法被观测到的变量。也无法通过传感器的数据和相关性来推断出这些变量（比如，红色飞机不会比白色飞机飞得更快）。所以，它们并非“被隐藏了”的变量，而只是根本无法被观测到的变量而已。如果在模型中加入了这种无法被观测到的变量，那么对该变量的估算结果将毫无意义。

What makes this possible? Imagine for a moment that we superimposed the velocity from a different airplane over the position graph. Clearly the two are not related, and there is no way that combining the two could possibly yield any additional information. In contrast, the velocity of this airplane tells us something very important - the direction and speed of travel. So long as the aircraft does not alter its velocity the velocity allows us to predict where the next position is. After a relatively small amount of error in velocity the probability that it is a good match with the position is very small. Think about it - if you suddenly change direction your position is also going to change a lot. If the measurement of the position is not in the direction of the velocity change it is very unlikely to be true. The two are correlated, so if the velocity changes so must the position, and in a predictable way.
是什么让这一点成为可能呢？试想一下，如果我们把另一架飞机的速度数据叠加在位置图上会怎样。显然，这两者之间没有任何关联，将它们结合起来也无法获得任何有用的信息。而实际上，这架飞机的速度数据却能告诉我们非常重要的信息——飞机的行进方向和速度。只要飞机的速度保持不变，我们就能根据其当前速度来预测它下一步会出现在哪里。如果速度的测量误差较大，那么该速度数据与实际位置的匹配度就会很低。想想看，如果你突然改变方向，那么你的位置也会发生很大变化。如果位置数据的测量方向与速度变化的方向不一致，那么这个数据就很可能是不准确的。这两者是相互关联的：只要速度发生变化，位置也必然会随之变化，而且这种变化是可预测的。

It is important to understand that we are taking advantage of the fact that velocity and position are correlated. We get a rough estimate of velocity from the distance and time between two measurements, and use Bayes theorem to produce very accurate estimates after only a few observations. Please reread this section if you have any doubts. If you do not understand this you will quickly find it impossible to reason about what you will learn in the following chapters.
重要的是要明白：我们利用了速度与位置之间存在关联这一事实。我们通过两次测量之间的距离和时间来大致估算速度，然后再利用贝叶斯定理，在进行少量观测后就能得到非常精确的估计值。如果您有任何疑问，请重新阅读这一部分。如果不理解这一点，那么您将很难理解后续章节的内容。

The effect of including velocity appears to me minor if only care about the position. But this is only after one update. In the next chapter we will see what a dramatic increase in certainty we have after multiple updates. The measurment variance will be large, but the estimated position variance will be small. Each time you intersect the velocity covariance with position it gets narrower on the x-axis, hence the variance is also smaller each time.
在我看来，如果只关心位置的话，考虑速度所带来的影响其实微乎其微。不过这只是在只进行一次更新之后的情况。在下一章中，我们将看到：经过多次更新后，我们的预测精度会有多么显著的提升。虽然测量结果的方差会很大，但估计出的位置方差则会很小。每次将速度的协方差与位置数据相结合后，x 轴上的方差都会变小，因此每次更新后的方差也会随之减小。

Higher Dimensions  更高维度¶

So far I have shown you two dimensional Gaussians, but the math does not limit you to two dimensions. In later chapters we will be working in 9, or even 12 dimensions. If you work in areas such as weather prediction, you can end up with thousands of dimensions.
到目前为止，我向你们展示了二维高斯分布，但实际上，数学原理并不局限于二维空间。在后面的章节中，我们将研究九维或十二维的空间。如果你从事天气预报等领域的工作，那么你可能会面对成千上万的维度。

What do these higher dimensions 'look like? Well, a two dimensional Gaussian can be represented by an error ellipse, so it stands to reason a three dimensional Gaussian could be represented by a 3D error ellipsoid. We won't delve into the math here, but this turns out to be true. FilterPy provides a function to plot this ellipse.
这些高维空间究竟“长什么样”呢？其实，二维高斯分布可以用误差椭圆来表示，因此，三维高斯分布自然可以用三维误差椭球来表示。这里就不详细讨论数学原理了，但事实证明确实如此。 FilterPy 提供了一个用于绘制这种椭球的函数。

First, let's make some noisy data with a given covariance, just so we can plot it inside the ellipsoid.
首先，让我们生成一些具有特定协方差的随机数据，这样我们就可以把它们绘制在椭球体内部了。

In [48]:  在[48]中：
from filterpy.stats import plot_3d_covariance

mu = [0.3, 5., 10.]
C = np.array([[1.0, .03, .2],
              [.03,  4.0, .0],
              [.2,  .0, 16.1]])

sample = np.random.multivariate_normal(mu, C, size=1000)

Now we plot the ellipsoid with the FilterPy function plot_3d_covariance, and then scatter plot the samples.
现在，我们使用 FilterPy 函数 plot_3d_covariance 来绘制椭球体，然后再将各个样本点以散点图的形式展示出来。

In [49]:  在[49]中：
ax = plot_3d_covariance(mu, C, alpha=.4, std=3, limit_xyz=True)
ax.scatter(sample[:, 0], sample[:, 1], zs=sample[:, 2],);

Theory states that roughly 99% of a distribution will fall within 3 standard deviations, and this appears to be the case.
根据理论，大约 99%的数据点会落在 3 个标准差范围内，实际情况似乎也确实如此。

Nine dimensions? I haven't quite figured out how to plot a 9D ellipsoid on a 2D screen, so there will be no graphs. The concept is the same; the standard deviation error of the distribution can be described by a 9D ellipsoid.
九个维度？我还不清楚该如何在二维屏幕上绘制出九维椭球体，所以无法展示相关的图表。不过其原理是一样的：该分布的标准差误差可以用九维椭球体来描述。

Summary  摘要/总结¶

We have taken advantage of the geometry and correlations of the system to produce a very accurate estimate. The math does not care whether we are working with two positions, or a position and a correlated velocity, or if these are spatial dimensions. If floor space is correlated to house price you can write a Kalman filter to track house prices. If age is correlated to disease incidence you can write a Kalman filter to track diseases. If the zombie population is inversely correlated with the number of shotguns then you can write a Kalman filter to track zombie populations. I showed you this in terms of geometry and talked about triangulation. That was just to build your intuition. You can write a Kalman filter for state variables that have no geometric representation, such as filters for stock prices or milk production of cows (I received an email from someone tracking milk production!) Get used to thinking of these as Gaussians with correlations. If we can express our uncertainties as a multidimensional Gaussian we can then multiply the prior with the likelihood and get a much more accurate result.
我们充分利用了该系统的几何特性和各种相关性，从而得到了非常精确的估算结果。在数学上，我们不必关心所处理的究竟是两个数值、一个数值与一个相关联的速度，还是其他类型的空间参数。如果房屋面积与房价之间存在相关性，那么就可以用卡尔曼滤波器来追踪房价变化。同样地，如果年龄与疾病发病率相关，也可以用卡尔曼滤波器来监测疾病趋势。如果僵尸数量与猎枪的数量呈负相关，那么同样可以用卡尔曼滤波器来追踪僵尸数量的变化。我之前用几何概念来解释这一点，还提到了三角测量法。这么做只是为了帮助大家更好地理解这个原理。其实，即使某些状态变量没有明确的几何表现形式，也可以用卡尔曼滤波器来处理，比如股票价格或牛奶产量等数据（我甚至收到过有人用卡尔曼滤波器来追踪牛奶产量的邮件！）试着把这些看作是具有相关性的高斯分布吧。如果我们能把各种不确定性表示为多维高斯分布，那么就可以将先验概率与似然值相乘，从而得到更精确的结果。

References  参考文献¶

  • [1] http://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/tutorial/stats.html
    [1] http://docs.scipy.org.cn/doc/scipy/reference/tutorial/stats.html

  • [2] FilterPy library. Roger Labbe. https://github.com/rlabbe/filterpy
    [2] FilterPy 库。Roger Labbe 开发。https://github.com/rlabbe/filterpy
